grades.label: Market Position
grades.evidence: 3M describes itself as “a diversified global manufacturer” and “among the leading manufacturers of products for many of the markets it serves,” with well‑known product franchises (Scotch®, Post‑it®, Thinsulate™, VHB™) and broad positions across five high‑margin business segments (each posting ~21%+ operating margins).

grades.label: Moat Type & Strength
grades.evidence: The filing emphasizes technology innovation, broad product breadth and global manufacturing scale (multiple leading product lines and serving many end markets), sustained R&D (R&D ~5.8% of sales) and high, consistent operating margins (22.9% in 2015), indicating reinforcing scale and manufacturing advantages.

grades.label: Market Quality
grades.evidence: 3M operates across a mix of industrial, health care, consumer and electronics markets that the filing treats as durable and diversified (organic local‑currency growth in Health Care and Consumer, and predictable cash flows and dividend track record), suggesting steady, predictable end‑market demand rather than a single secular boom or structural decline.

grades.label: Leadership Quality
grades.evidence: Capital allocation: management repurchased “more than $5 billion of its own stock each year” and the Board authorized a $10 billion repurchase program while also making targeted acquisitions (Capital Safety, Polypore, Ivera), indicating active capital deployment but with higher leverage (debt/total capital rose to 48% in 2015). Strategic clarity: the company outlines a coherent multi‑year business transformation (ERP implementation), clear segment structure and a stated focus on technology innovation and disruptive R&D initiatives. Execution track record: results show sustained, high operating margins (~22%), consistent operating cash flow ($6.4 billion in 2015) and use of restructuring to manage costs, though 2015 organic growth slowed and FX impacted results, consistent with competent execution overall.
